track.title: Chama Investment Basics
track.description: A chama route for contribution discipline, records, investment policy, land checks, minutes, voting, and accountability.

course.title: Chama Investment Basics: Core route
course.description: Group-money basics before a chama buys, lends, or invests.

lessons.title: What makes a chama strong?
lessons.summary: A strong chama has a clear purpose, written rules, records, and decision discipline.
lessons.introduction: A chama is strongest when members agree the purpose before money accumulates. Trust matters, but trust without records can become conflict when amounts grow.
sections: Start with the goal: welfare, saving, lending, asset buying, or long-term investment.
Write contribution rules, penalties, voting thresholds, signatories, and exit rules.
Keep records that a new member or auditor could understand later.

scenario.title: Ten members, one goal
scenario.text: Ten members contribute KES 5,000 monthly. Before buying land, they agree what documents must be verified and who can approve spending.

lessons.mistake: Depending only on friendship while leaving money rules vague.
lessons.action: Write the chama purpose in one sentence and list the top three rules.
lessons.takeaway: Group trust becomes stronger when rules are clear.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Use professional legal or tax review for complex group decisions.

lessons.title: Contribution discipline
lessons.summary: Contribution discipline protects fairness and future planning.
lessons.introduction: A chama plan can fail if contributions are irregular and rules are applied emotionally. Discipline makes the group's cash position and member balances easier to trust.
sections: Agree contribution amount, due date, grace period, and penalties before problems appear.
Record payments and balances in a shared but secure format.
Separate welfare money from investment money if the group has both purposes.

scenario.title: Late contribution month
scenario.text: Two members miss a deadline. The chama follows the written rule instead of arguing in the chat group.

lessons.mistake: Changing contribution rules depending on who is late.
lessons.action: Create a contribution record template with member, date, amount, purpose, and balance.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Keep private member information controlled and shared only with consent.

lessons.title: Chama investment policy
lessons.summary: A simple investment policy says what the group can and cannot do.
lessons.introduction: A chama investment policy is a written guide for decisions. It reduces rushed voting and protects members from pressure when a tempting deal appears.
sections: The policy should list allowed asset types, maximum exposure, minimum cash buffer, and approval process.
It should say when professional review is required, especially for land, tax, legal documents, or complex products.
It should define what information must be checked before money leaves the account.

scenario.title: Land offer in the group
scenario.text: A member brings a land offer. The chama checks whether the policy allows land, what documents are required, and how many votes are needed.

lessons.mistake: Voting on a deal before agreeing the decision rules.
lessons.action: Draft one rule for cash buffer, one rule for approvals, and one rule for professional review.
lessons.takeaway: Policy protects the group from excitement.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Chama policy is editorial education; legal documents should be reviewed by qualified professionals.

lessons.title: Chama land due diligence
lessons.summary: Land decisions need documents, independent checks, and no deposit pressure.
lessons.introduction: Land can be emotionally attractive for groups, but it can also create fraud, tax, legal, and liquidity issues. A chama should verify before deposit and record every approval.
sections: Do not pay a deposit before independent document and seller checks.
Use legal review, survey checks, site visits, official searches, and tax/stamp-duty questions.
Agree who holds documents and how members can inspect them.

scenario.title: Deposit deadline
scenario.text: A seller says another group is ready to pay. The chama refuses urgency and completes independent verification first.

lessons.mistake: Sending a deposit to hold land before proving ownership and authority to sell.
lessons.action: Create a due-diligence checklist before any member views land.
lessons.takeaway: Land due diligence is a process, not a WhatsApp vote.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Verify tax and transfer questions with KRA, land professionals, and legal advisers; PesaRoute does not give tax or legal advice.

lessons.title: Minutes, voting, and accountability
lessons.summary: Good minutes and voting records protect both leaders and members.
lessons.introduction: Minutes are not paperwork for its own sake. They record who decided what, what information was available, what money moved, and who is accountable.
sections: Minutes should capture attendance, agenda, documents reviewed, motions, votes, approvals, and action owners.
Voting thresholds should be written before a large decision.
Members should be able to review decisions without exposing unnecessary private information.

scenario.title: Treasurer handover
scenario.text: A treasurer changes jobs and leaves the group. Clear minutes and records make handover less risky.

lessons.mistake: Relying on memory or chat messages for large money decisions.
lessons.action: Write a sample minute for one decision: amount, reason, vote, documents, and next action.
lessons.takeaway: Accountability is easier when records are calm and complete.
lessons.source_note: Source note: Use legal or professional review for formal group structures where needed.

quiz_questions.prompt: What should a chama policy include?
options: Allowed decisions, approvals, cash buffer, and review rules
quiz_questions.correct_answer: Allowed decisions, approvals, cash buffer, and review rules
quiz_questions.explanation: A policy protects group money from rushed decisions.

quiz_questions.prompt: What should happen before a land deposit?
quiz_questions.explanation: Land documents and seller authority should be checked before payment.

flashcards.back: Clear purpose, written rules, records, and decision discipline.
To protect fairness, balances, and accountability.

flashcards.front: What is a chama investment policy?
flashcards.back: A written guide for allowed decisions and approval rules.
flashcards.example: Professional review required before land purchase.

flashcards.front: What is a land deposit red flag?
flashcards.back: Pressure to pay before independent verification.
flashcards.example: Seller says the offer closes today.

flashcards.back: Attendance, documents, motion, vote, amount, and action owner.
flashcards.example: Approved KES range for survey check.

resources.title: Chama investment policy checklist
resources.body: A checklist for chama purpose, contribution rules, cash buffer, allowed assets, voting thresholds, document storage, professional review, and exit rules.
items: Require review for land or complex decisions.

resources.title: Land before deposit checklist
resources.body: A land-before-deposit checklist for groups and individuals: seller identity, title/search, survey, site visit, legal review, taxes, approvals, and no pressure payments.
items: Do not pay before independent verification.
